article_items.title: TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 - Overview
article_items.description: TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 is the startup epicenter for tech and VC leaders. Taking place at Moscone West in San Francisco from October 13-15, 2026.
article_items.excerpt: TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 is the startup epicenter for tech and VC leaders. Taking place at Moscone West in San Francisco from October 13-15, 2026.
article_items.summary: Before founding Wiz, Assaf was the founder and CEO of the security company Adallom, which was acquired by Microsoft for $320 million in 2015. The practical impact: He became CFO in 2001 and led the company through both its IPO in early 2002 and the subsequent acquisition by eBay. In 2022, he became a New York Times bestselling author for his acclaimed children’s picture book, “I Color Myself Different.” He will release his next children’s book, “We Are Free, You and Me” in October 2024, co-authored with his wife Nessa and published in partnership with Scholastic. article_items.title: Startup Battlefield 200 applications close before May 27 | TechCrunch
article_items.description: The deadline to apply or nominate for Startup Battlefield 200 is May 27. Get VC access, global visibility, TechCrunch coverage, and $100,000. Apply now.
article_items.excerpt: The deadline to apply or nominate for Startup Battlefield 200 is May 27. Get VC access, global visibility, TechCrunch coverage, and $100,000. Apply now.
article_items.summary: The network runs so deep that alumni have even acquired each other: Dropbox acquired fellow Battlefield 200 alum DocSend in 2021. The companies that define categories rarely start polished Some of the most consequential companies in tech history didn’t launch with splashy fundraising announcements. The practical impact: You don’t need to make the top 20 for this experience to change your trajectory. This is your opportunity to gain investor exposure, receive direct VC feedback, and prove your company belongs among the next generation of category-defining startups. Watch next: Startup Battlefield 200 has never been a competition for the most polished companies. The deadline to apply or nominate for Startup Battlefield 200 is May 27.

key_points: The network runs so deep that alumni have even acquired each other: Dropbox acquired fellow Battlefield 200 alum DocSend in 2021.
The deadline to apply or nominate for Startup Battlefield 200 is May 27.
Apply today for the opportunity to take the TechCrunch Disrupt Stage alongside 200 of the world’s most promising early-stage startups.
The companies that define categories rarely start polished Some of the most consequential companies in tech history didn’t launch with splashy fundraising announcements.
They all passed through the same crucible: Startup Battlefield 200.
Together, they’ve raised over $32 billion and generated more than 250 exits, including acquisitions by Microsoft, Google, Salesforce, Uber, and Amazon.
Startup Battlefield 200 has never been a competition for the most polished companies.
The Startup Battlefield 200 track record speaks for itself More than 1,700 companies have competed in Startup Battlefield 200.
One startup takes the crown and wins $100,000 in equity-free funding.
More from TechCrunch Startup Battlefield 200 applications close in days: Apply before May 27 The deadline to apply or nominate for Startup Battlefield 200 is Friday, May 27.
Just 20 finalists pitch live on the Disrupt Stage.
If you’re building something category-defining - or know a startup that deserves the spotlight - submit your nomination and complete your application before Friday, May 27.

article_items.description: We’re on a journey to advance and democratize artificial intelligence through open source and open science.
article_items.excerpt: We’re on a journey to advance and democratize artificial intelligence through open source and open science.
article_items.summary: These four are specific to training, where the agent runs through tasks, gets scored, and its model's weights get updated. In the LLM world, the term has expanded. key_points: @Vtrivedy10: The Anatomy of an Agent Harness : detailed breakdown of harness components and why each exists Agent Harness Engineering : convergent framing on Agent = Model + Harness, with coding agent examples...
These four are specific to training, where the agent runs through tasks, gets scored, and its model's weights get updated.
In the LLM world, the term has expanded.
The trainer is what makes the agent better: it runs many agent episodes, scores the results and uses them to update the inner model's weights.

At evaluation time, the same pattern shows up as an eval harness : instead of collecting training data, it runs a fixed set of scenarios at a model checkpoint and records metrics rather than updating weights.
If you're not the model, you're the harness.
It shapes how the model sees the world and acts in it, whether during training or at inference.
The score that tells the training algorithm whether the model is getting better.
The harness is what makes the agent run.
The model, the harness, and the product are three different things.
Wrap a checkpoint in scaffolding and a harness and deploy it, and you get an agent whose behavior is the policy.
At training time, the harness also runs many of these loops in parallel and feeds the results back to update the model.

article_items.title: Everyone is navigating AI security in real time -- even Google | TechCrunch
article_items.description: We're in the transition period -- all of us.

article_items.summary: He noted that the average time between an initial breach and the handoff to the next stage of an attack has dropped from eight hours to 22 seconds, and that the attack surface has expanded well beyond the traditional network perimeter. The practical impact: It’s not just a security team’s issue.” But even as AI takes on more of the defensive workload, the people qualified to oversee it are in short supply - and the vulnerabilities that AI itself is introducing are multiplying faster than security teams can address them.
